prompt: What is a 'seam' in the context of extensibility?
choices: A bug that appears only when two modules are combined
A deliberate point where new behavior can be added without rewriting the surrounding code
A performance bottleneck between two layers
A naming convention for configuration files

prompt: According to the YAGNI principle, when should you AVOID adding a seam?
choices: When the change is both likely and expensive to retrofit later
When the future change is speculative and would be cheap to add later anyway
Whenever a boundary exists between two modules
Whenever more than one developer works on the code

prompt: A team expects to support several payment providers over time. Where should they place a seam?
choices: Inside the database schema for orders
At the payment boundary, as a common interface each provider implements
In the UI button that starts checkout

prompt: Select ALL true statements about extensibility seams.
choices: A well-placed seam lets new behavior slot in without editing existing, working code
Every seam is free and adds no cost to the codebase
Adding a seam for every imaginable future can lead to over-engineering that is harder to change
Seams should be placed along the axis where change is most likely to occur
